title: Syracuse and Onondaga county leaders report progress on lead paint remediation
summary: Syracuse and Onondaga County leadership report progress on a joint lead paint remediation initiative targeting the city's aging housing stock. Only 1% of 541 tested preschoolers showed elevated blood lead levels; 72% of 8,000+ code violations have been addressed, with the program also placing foster families in 12 remediated homes. County Executive McMahon noted mixed results, with further testing pending.

political_signal.description: Leadership highlighting cross-agency coordination on public health initiative with measurable outcomes; County Executive's 'mixed' characterization suggests cautious optimism requiring continued monitoring and investment.

processing_notes: No dollar amounts specified in article; budget implications unknown
Testing data limited to preschool population; broader residential testing referenced but results pending
72% violation closure rate represents significant operational progress but leaves 28% unresolved
Foster family placement component suggests equity-focused implementation approach
